items.name: Water Stain on Ceiling
items.description: Visible brown or dark discoloration on the ceiling suggesting past or active moisture intrusion.
items.embed_text: Water stain on ceiling. Brown, dark, or ring-shaped ceiling discoloration suggesting moisture intrusion. Investigate leak source and repair stained ceiling finish.

items.name: Peeling or Discolored Paint
items.description: Paint that is peeling, bubbling, cracked, or visibly aged.
items.embed_text: Peeling or discolored paint. Flaking, bubbling, cracked, aged paint. Lower-wall moisture staining, tide marks, and discoloration near baseboards or base of walls. Prep and repaint.

items.name: Visible Mold or Mildew
items.description: Dark, fuzzy, or patchy growth on surfaces indicative of active or past moisture problems.
items.embed_text: Visible mold or mildew. Dark, fuzzy, patchy, or organic-looking growth on surfaces suggesting moisture-related microbial staining. Remediate affected material and address the moisture source.

items.name: Drywall Damage or Cracks
items.description: Cracks, holes, nail pops, or uneven drywall indicating settling, impact damage, or poor repairs.
items.embed_text: Drywall damage or cracks. Holes, cracks, nail pops, rough patches, missing sections, or deteriorated wallboard. Patch, finish, and repaint affected drywall.

items.name: Ceiling Cracks or Sagging
items.description: Cracks, separation at joints, or sagging areas in ceiling indicating movement or moisture damage.
items.embed_text: Ceiling cracks or sagging. Separation, sagging ceiling areas. Exposed ceiling joists or framing, missing ceiling panels or sections, open unfinished ceiling, incomplete ceiling finish. Investigate and repair ceiling/drywall.

items.name: Damaged or Sagging Soffit/Porch Ceiling Panels
items.description: Patchy, misaligned, or deteriorated ceiling panels under an overhang or covered entry. Sagging porch ceiling, loose soffit panels, or missing soffit sections.
items.embed_text: Damaged or sagging soffit or porch ceiling panels. Loose, missing, misaligned, or deteriorated panels under an eave, overhang, carport, or covered entry. Repair or replace affected exterior ceiling panels.

items.name: Worn or Stained Carpet
items.description: Carpet that is visibly dirty, stained, frayed, or heavily worn.
items.embed_text: Worn or stained carpet. Dirty, matted, stained, frayed, or visibly aged carpet that may need replacement.

items.name: Worn or Stained Vinyl / Linoleum Flooring
items.description: Vinyl sheet, linoleum, or resilient flooring that is visibly stained, discolored, worn through, or peeling.
items.embed_text: Worn or stained vinyl or linoleum flooring. Stained, discolored, torn, peeling, or deteriorated resilient flooring. Replace or resurface affected flooring.

items.name: Scratched or Damaged Hard Flooring
items.description: Hardwood/laminate/vinyl/tile with scratches, chips, warping, broken tiles, or visible damage.
items.embed_text: Scratched or damaged hard flooring. Scratches, scuffs, chips, warping, broken pieces, or failed floor finish. Repair, refinish, or replace affected flooring.

items.name: Trip Hazard or Unlevel Floor
items.description: Uneven flooring, loose thresholds, or abrupt height changes posing a trip risk or indicating settlement.
items.embed_text: Trip hazard or unlevel floor. Uneven floors, loose thresholds, height changes. Correct flooring/transition.

items.name: Damaged or Rotted Exterior Siding/Trim
items.description: Exterior siding/trim showing rot, warping, cracks, missing sections, or deterioration.
items.embed_text: Damaged or rotted exterior siding or trim. Rot, warping, cracking, missing sections. Rotted door bottom, water damage at door base, deteriorated door sill or frame, door frame rot. Exterior repair.

items.name: Damaged or Aged Roof Shingles
items.description: Missing, curling, patchy, worn shingles or debris suggesting reduced remaining life.
items.embed_text: Damaged or aged roof shingles. Missing, curling, or worn shingles. Roof surface discoloration, debris-covered or dirty shingles, moss or algae growth on roof, patched or uneven roof surface. Heavy leaf or debris buildup on roof surface. Roof repair.

items.name: Damaged or Unsafe Deck/Porch
items.description: Deck/porch/stairs with loose, rotted, or missing boards or railings appearing unsafe.
items.embed_text: Damaged or unsafe deck or porch. Loose, rotted, missing, or deteriorated boards, railings, stairs, landings, or porch surfaces. Repair or replace affected exterior walking surfaces.

items.name: Broken or Fogged Windows
items.description: Cracked glass, damaged frames, missing panes, or fogged double-pane windows indicating failed seals.
items.embed_text: Broken or fogged windows. Cracked glass, fogged panes, failed seals, damaged frames. Window repair or replacement.

items.name: Dirty or Grimy Window Screens
items.description: Heavy buildup of grime, dirt, or debris on window screens and frames. Screens needing cleaning or replacement.
items.embed_text: Dirty or grimy window screens. Screens or frames have heavy dirt, grime, or debris buildup. Clean or replace screens as needed.

items.name: Dated or Older Windows
items.description: Older single-hung or sliding window frames, dated window style, or aging window units. Window replacement opportunity for improved energy efficiency and appearance.
items.embed_text: Dated or older windows. Window units, frames, or operating styles appear aged or obsolete. Window replacement may improve energy efficiency and appearance.

items.name: Damaged or Outdated Cabinets
items.description: Cabinets with visible wear, damaged doors/drawers, water staining, or dated style.
items.embed_text: Damaged or outdated cabinets. Worn or dated cabinetry, damaged doors/drawers. Cabinet repair, refinish, or replacement.

items.name: Damaged Countertops
items.description: Countertops with burns, chips, cracks, swollen seams, or missing sections.
items.embed_text: Damaged countertops. Chips, cracks, burns, swollen seams. Countertop replacement.

items.name: Damaged Tile or Grout
items.description: Broken/loose/missing tile or grout that may allow moisture intrusion.
items.embed_text: Damaged tile or grout. Cracked, loose, missing, stained, or deteriorated tile or grout. Repair tile, grout, or caulk to restore a durable moisture-resistant surface.

items.name: Appliance Damage or Missing
items.description: Key appliances missing, visibly broken, significantly aged or neglected.
items.embed_text: Appliance damage or missing. Missing/broken/aged kitchen appliances. Replace appliances.

items.name: Leaking or Stained Plumbing Fixtures
items.description: Leaks, corrosion, staining, or mineral buildup at sinks/toilets/tubs/showers.
items.embed_text: Leaking or stained plumbing fixtures. Leaks, corrosion, staining, buildup at sink/toilet/tub/shower. Plumbing repair.

items.name: Stained or Damaged Bath Fixtures
items.description: Tubs/showers/toilets with heavy staining, chipped enamel, or worn finishes.
items.embed_text: Stained or damaged bath fixtures. Worn finishes, staining, chipped enamel. Shower pan or base perimeter grime, staining at floor edges, worn bath enclosure edges. Fixture update or replacement.

items.name: Visible Electrical Risks
items.description: Exposed wiring, missing covers, scorch marks, overloaded strips, unsafe components.
items.embed_text: Visible electrical risks. Exposed wiring, missing outlet covers, scorch marks, overloaded power strips. Bare bulb on hanging socket, temporary hanging light, unfinished light connection, fixture wiring exposed from ceiling. Electrical repair.

items.name: Missing or Damaged Handrails/Guardrails
items.description: Missing/loose/broken railings on stairs, balconies, decks.
items.embed_text: Missing or damaged handrails or guardrails. Loose/broken/missing railings. Safety repair.

items.name: Basement or Garage Damage
items.description: Cracking, moisture intrusion, efflorescence, or structural wear in basement/garage walls or floors.
items.embed_text: Basement or garage damage. Cracks, efflorescence, moisture staining, surface deterioration, or structural wear in lower-level or garage areas. Evaluate and repair moisture or structural issues.

items.name: Major Foundation or Settlement Signs
items.description: Large step cracks, sloped floors, misaligned doors/windows, signs of notable settlement.
items.embed_text: Major foundation or settlement signs. Step cracks, sloped floors, misaligned doors/windows. Foundation/structure evaluation.

items.name: Visible Pest or Rodent Evidence
items.description: Droppings, chewed materials, nests, or clear signs of insects/rodents.
items.embed_text: Visible pest or rodent evidence. Droppings, nests, chewed materials. Pest control and cleanup.

items.name: Standing Water or Poor Grading
items.description: Standing water, negative grading, saturated soil near structure increasing moisture risk.
items.embed_text: Standing water or poor grading. Water pooling, negative grading near foundation. Drainage and grading correction.

items.name: Driveway or Walkway Cracking/Settlement
items.description: Cracks, heaving, spalling, or settlement in concrete/paved areas impacting safety or curb appeal.
items.embed_text: Driveway or walkway cracking/settlement. Cracks, heaving, spalling. Uneven, settled, heaved, displaced, or cracked concrete, stone, paver, or flagstone surfaces at driveways, walkways, entry paths, or exterior transition areas. Concrete repair.

items.name: Clogged or Damaged Gutters/Downspouts
items.description: Gutters/downspouts clogged, sagging, disconnected, or dumping water near foundation.
items.embed_text: Clogged or damaged gutters/downspouts. Sagging, clogged, disconnected gutters. Gutter repair/cleaning.

items.name: Trees or Vegetation Too Close to Structure
items.description: Vegetation contacting or very close to siding/roof/foundation promoting moisture, pests, or damage.
items.embed_text: Trees or vegetation too close to structure. Overgrowth contacting siding/roof. Trim and clear vegetation.

items.name: Outdated Kitchen Finishes
items.description: Older cabinetry, counters, appliances, or finishes that would benefit from modernization.
items.embed_text: Outdated kitchen finishes. Dated cabinetry, countertops, appliances, hardware, backsplash, or mixed kitchen finishes. Kitchen modernization or finish update.

items.name: Outdated Bathroom Finishes
items.description: Older tile, vanity, fixtures, or layout where an update would improve appeal.
items.embed_text: Outdated bathroom finishes. Dated tile, vanity, fixtures. Worn laminate vanity top, aged sink cabinet, deteriorated vanity edges, dated bath cabinet surfaces. Worn countertop edges in bathroom, dated faucet or hardware. Bathroom update.

items.name: Outdated Flooring Style
items.description: Flooring is clean but visually outdated (older carpet, laminate, linoleum, dated tile).
items.embed_text: Outdated flooring style. Intact flooring that appears visually dated, including older carpet, laminate, linoleum, vinyl, or tile. Flooring update for marketability.

items.name: Outdated Lighting Fixtures
items.description: Older ceiling lights, fans, sconces, or track lighting where modern fixtures improve the look.
items.embed_text: Outdated lighting fixtures. Older, builder-grade, ornate, fluorescent, or visibly dated ceiling lights, fans, chandeliers, or sconces. Replace fixtures to modernize the space.

items.name: Dated Bathroom Vanity Light
items.description: Dated or builder-grade bathroom vanity light fixture above mirror or sink area.
items.embed_text: Dated bathroom vanity light. Three-bulb vanity bar, builder-grade vanity fixture, curved arm vanity fixture with frosted shades, basic bathroom light over mirror, Hollywood-style vanity strip light, outdated bath sconce. Vanity lighting update.

items.name: Paint Refresh Opportunity
items.description: Older or highly personalized colors where repainting in modern neutrals would improve appeal.
items.embed_text: Paint refresh recommended. Interior paint looks aged, yellowed, heavily personalized, or dated. Repaint with a clean modern finish.

items.name: Landscape Improvement
items.description: Exterior/yard appears sparse, overgrown, or tired; could improve with landscaping cleanup.
items.embed_text: Landscape improvement. Yard cleanup, trimming, debris removal, refresh for curb appeal. Dirt or debris buildup around exterior areas, general site cleanup needed. Patchy, sparse, or bare lawn, thin turf, dormant grass areas needing overseeding or sod.

items.name: Curb Appeal Upgrade
items.description: Entry/porch/facade could benefit from cosmetic updates (paint, hardware, accents).
items.embed_text: Curb appeal upgrade. Exterior entry, porch, facade, or accent details appear tired, dated, plain, or uninviting. Exterior cosmetic updates may improve first impression.

items.name: Unfinished Basement
items.description: Unfinished basement suggests opportunity for added space through future finishing.
items.embed_text: Unfinished basement. Bare concrete floor, stripped basement floor, unfinished utility surfaces, exposed concrete slab in basement. Opportunity to finish space and add living area.

items.name: Staging or Decluttering Opportunity
items.description: Rooms appear cluttered/personalized; decluttering or staging improves perception.
items.embed_text: Staging or decluttering opportunity. Clean up clutter to improve presentation.

items.name: Baseboard Wear or Scuffs
items.description: Baseboards show scuffs, paint loss, dents, or general wear that reduces perceived condition.
items.embed_text: Baseboard wear or scuffs. Scuffed, dented, or chipped baseboards and trim. Missing baseboards, detached trim, visible gaps at wall base, incomplete base trim installation. Repair, repaint, or replace trim.

items.name: Wall Scuffs, Marks, or Dents
items.description: Walls show visible scuffs, stains, dents, or minor damage indicating heavy use or deferred maintenance.
items.embed_text: Wall scuffs, marks, dents, or localized staining. Visible wall wear such as scuffs, smudges, dents, dirty areas, or minor staining. Patch, prep, and repaint as needed.

items.name: Ceiling Fan with Mismatched Blades
items.description: Ceiling fan has mismatched or damaged blades, indicating a repair issue and reduced visual appeal.
items.embed_text: Ceiling fan with mismatched blades. Fan blades mismatched/damaged. Replace fan or blades.

items.name: Outdated Ceiling Fan Style
items.description: Ceiling fan appears older/builder-grade; a modern replacement improves lighting and overall look.
items.embed_text: Outdated ceiling fan style. Older/builder-grade ceiling fan. Upgrade to modern fan/fixture.

items.name: Popcorn/Acoustic Ceiling Texture
items.description: Ceiling has popcorn/acoustic texture; smoothing or re-texturing modernizes the interior.
items.embed_text: Popcorn or acoustic ceiling texture. Stippled, spray-textured, acoustic, or heavy textured ceiling finish that appears dated. Modernize by smoothing or re-texturing.

items.name: Unfinished Interior Wall (OSB Exposed)
items.description: Exposed OSB/sheathing on an interior wall indicates an unfinished or incomplete wall assembly requiring proper finishing.
items.embed_text: Unfinished interior wall with exposed OSB. Interior wall not finished (OSB/sheathing visible). Frame/drywall/finish.

items.name: Dirty or Heavily Soiled Floor
items.description: Floor appears visibly dirty or heavily soiled, reducing perceived condition and possibly indicating deeper wear.
items.embed_text: Dirty or heavily soiled floor. Visible grime, mud, tracked-in dirt, darkened grout, or widespread staining on floor surfaces. Clean, restore, and evaluate for replacement if needed.

items.name: Vintage Tile Pattern/Style
items.description: Tile pattern/style reads as vintage or dated; updating tile can modernize the bathroom.
items.embed_text: Vintage tile pattern/style. Dated bathroom tile pattern. Update tile for a modern look.

items.name: Ornate/Vintage Mirror Style
items.description: Ornate or vintage-style mirror (e.g., oval ornate frame) reads dated; swapping improves appeal.
items.embed_text: Ornate/vintage mirror style. Oval ornate framed mirror. Replace with modern mirror/vanity setup.

items.name: Outdated or Damaged Vanity
items.description: Bathroom vanity is worn, damaged, or visibly outdated with deteriorated finishes, doors, or hardware.
items.embed_text: Outdated or damaged vanity. Worn bathroom vanity with peeling laminate, water-damaged base, swollen particle board, broken hinges or drawer fronts. Dated vanity styling such as oak, golden oak, builder-grade flat-panel, or 1990s-style cabinetry. Replace bathroom vanity.

items.name: Tub Surround or Shower Pan Damage
items.description: Cracked, chipped, or visibly damaged tub surround, shower pan, or shower base allowing potential moisture intrusion.
items.embed_text: Tub surround or shower pan damage. Cracked fiberglass tub, chipped enamel surface, separated tub surround panels, damaged shower base or pan, deteriorated shower walls, broken or warped shower kit. Tub or shower replacement or repair to restore waterproof envelope.

items.name: Bathroom Plumbing Visible Issue
items.description: Visible bathroom plumbing concern: leaking supply lines, corroded shutoffs, failing drain assembly, or compromised P-trap.
items.embed_text: Bathroom plumbing visible issue. Leaking supply line under vanity, corroded shutoff valves, failing or improvised drain assembly, missing P-trap, rusted or staining bathroom plumbing. Active drip at sink or toilet supply, water on cabinet base from plumbing concern. Plumbing repair required.

items.name: Bathroom GFCI Missing or Damaged
items.description: Bathroom outlets near sink or wet area lack GFCI protection, or existing GFCI is damaged or unresponsive.
items.embed_text: Bathroom GFCI missing or damaged. Standard non-GFCI receptacle near bathroom sink, exposed wiring at bathroom outlet, damaged or cracked outlet face, missing GFCI protection on a wet-area circuit. Replace with code-compliant GFCI receptacle.

items.name: Active Water Damage in Bathroom
items.description: Active or recent water damage in bathroom: standing water, wet drywall, swollen subflooring, or fresh staining indicating ongoing moisture intrusion.
items.embed_text: Active water damage in bathroom. Standing water on bathroom floor, soft or buckled drywall near tub or shower, swollen or rotted baseboard, soggy or sunken subflooring, fresh staining on ceiling below bathroom. Moisture remediation and source repair required to halt active intrusion.

items.name: Mold or Mildew Visible in Bathroom
items.description: Visible mold or mildew growth in bathroom on tile, grout, caulk, ceiling, or drywall, suggesting persistent moisture or ventilation issues.
items.embed_text: Mold or mildew visible in bathroom. Black or dark organic staining on grout lines, tile corners, shower caulk, bathroom ceiling, or near exhaust fan. Microbial growth in tub surround or shower stall. Mold remediation and moisture or ventilation source repair.

items.name: Missing Bathroom Tile Exposed Substrate
items.description: Missing bathroom tile exposing substrate, backer board, or wall framing - severe failure of waterproof envelope.
items.embed_text: Missing bathroom tile exposed substrate. Large area of missing tile exposing cement board, drywall, or wall framing behind shower or tub. Section of tile torn off shower wall or bathroom floor leaving bare substrate. Significant tile failure indicating water intrusion and full tile replacement required.

items.name: Missing Vanity Exposed Plumbing
items.description: Bathroom vanity has been removed or is missing, leaving plumbing supply and drain lines exposed and unsupported.
items.embed_text: Missing vanity with exposed plumbing. Bathroom vanity removed exposing supply lines and P-trap, no sink cabinet present, stripped vanity area showing rough plumbing, pedestal or wall-hung sink removed leaving open plumbing. Vanity replacement and finished plumbing connection required.

items.name: Vanity Countertop Damage
items.description: Damaged bathroom vanity countertop with cracks, chips, burns, or deep staining.
items.embed_text: Vanity countertop damage. Cracked, chipped, burned, or deeply stained bathroom vanity countertop. Cultured marble vanity top with breaks or yellowing, laminate vanity top with delamination or burns. Vanity top replacement or repair.

items.name: Missing or Damaged Caulk at Tub or Shower
items.description: Failed, missing, or moldy caulk at tub edges, shower transitions, or vanity backsplash - needs re-caulking to prevent moisture intrusion.
items.embed_text: Missing or damaged caulk at tub or shower. Failed, separated, or visibly moldy caulk along tub edge, shower corners, tile-to-wall transitions, or vanity backsplash. Strip and re-caulk to restore moisture seal.

items.name: Bathroom Exhaust Fan Missing or Damaged
items.description: Bathroom exhaust fan is missing, broken, or visibly aged or grime-clogged - needed for moisture management and code compliance.
items.embed_text: Bathroom exhaust fan missing or damaged. No visible exhaust fan in bathroom, broken or dangling exhaust fan cover, grime-clogged fan housing, fan that does not appear operational. Install or replace bathroom exhaust fan.

items.name: Peeling or Damaged Bathroom Paint
items.description: Bathroom-specific paint failure: peeling near tub, bubbling near shower, moisture-related discoloration, or efflorescence on bathroom walls.
items.embed_text: Peeling or damaged bathroom paint. Paint failure near bathroom tub or shower, bubbling or flaking paint on bathroom walls or ceiling, moisture-related discoloration or efflorescence near plumbing fixtures. Prep, prime with moisture-resistant primer, and repaint with bathroom-rated paint.

items.name: Worn or Damaged Bathroom Flooring
items.description: Bathroom flooring damaged by moisture: lifted vinyl, soft subfloor near toilet, cracked floor tile, or buckled laminate.
items.embed_text: Worn or damaged bathroom flooring. Lifted or peeling vinyl tile near toilet base, soft or spongy subfloor at vanity or tub, cracked or missing bathroom floor tile, buckled or water-damaged laminate. Bathroom flooring replacement with moisture-rated material.

items.name: Dated or Worn Vanity Countertop
items.description: Vanity countertop is intact but visually dated: yellowed cultured marble, dated laminate pattern, or worn synthetic top.
items.embed_text: Dated or worn vanity countertop. Yellowed cultured marble, dated laminate vanity top, worn integral sink top, builder-grade synthetic top. Bathroom vanity countertop update for marketability.

items.name: Dated Bathroom Flooring Style
items.description: Bathroom flooring is intact but visually dated - older vinyl pattern, dated linoleum, mid-century tile, or builder-grade carpeting.
items.embed_text: Dated bathroom flooring style. Older vinyl pattern, dated linoleum, mid-century or vintage tile, builder-grade bathroom carpeting. Floor is intact but stylistically out of date. Bathroom flooring update for marketability.

items.name: Stained-Glass or Vintage Light Fixture
items.description: Stained-glass or vintage-style light fixture reads dated; replacing can modernize the space.
items.embed_text: Stained-glass or vintage light fixture. Dated lighting style. Replace with modern fixture.

items.name: Dated Window Treatment/Valance
items.description: Vintage-style valance or dated window treatments reduce perceived condition; updating improves appeal.
items.embed_text: Basic or builder-grade mini blinds, horizontal blinds, older blinds, worn window coverings. Dated window treatment or valance. Vintage window valance or curtains. Aged, bent, or damaged mini blinds, outdated horizontal blinds. Update treatments for a modern look.

items.name: Dated Wallpaper
items.description: Wallpaper appears dated or visually busy; removal and repainting improves market appeal.
items.embed_text: Dated wallpaper present. Old/patterned wallpaper. Remove and repaint walls.

items.name: Dated Wood Paneling
items.description: Wood paneling appears dated; painting, removing, or replacing can modernize the room.
items.embed_text: Dated wood paneling. Wood-paneled walls that make the room look dated, including dark, faux, knotty pine, or floor-to-ceiling paneling. Update by painting, removing, or replacing paneling.

items.name: Dated Fireplace Surround
items.description: Fireplace surround, mantel, hearth, or facing material appears dated; updating it can modernize the living area.
items.embed_text: Dated fireplace surround. Fireplace mantel, hearth, brick, stone, tile, or facing material appears stylistically dated. Update surround finishes to modernize the living area.

items.name: Mismatched Furniture/Staging Opportunity
items.description: Furniture and decor appear mismatched or distracting; staging improvements can improve perception and photos.
items.embed_text: Mismatched furniture/staging opportunity. Distracting or inconsistent decor. Improve staging for listing photos.

items.name: Overall Decor Style Appears Dated
items.description: Overall decor/finish selections feel dated; targeted cosmetic modernization may increase appeal.
items.embed_text: Overall decor style appears dated. Multiple visible finishes, fixtures, trim, doors, built-ins, or decor choices read as older or stylistically tired. Targeted cosmetic modernization may improve appeal.

items.name: Yard Debris / Overgrown with Leaves
items.description: Landscaping appears overgrown with fallen leaves or debris; cleanup improves curb appeal and drainage performance.
items.embed_text: Yard debris or overgrown with leaves. Leaf accumulation on ground, debris-covered yard, leaf litter along foundation, dead leaves and organic debris on walkways or yard surface. Landscaping overgrown and littered with leaves. Cleanup and landscape refresh.

items.name: Possible Water Damage at Roofline
items.description: Discoloration, staining, or deterioration near the roofline suggests possible moisture intrusion requiring evaluation.
items.embed_text: Possible water damage at roofline. Staining/deterioration at eaves/fascia/soffit. Inspect and repair leak source.

items.name: Gutter Maintenance Needed
items.description: Gutters/downspouts appear to need cleaning, inspection, or minor service to ensure proper drainage.
items.embed_text: Gutter maintenance needed. Gutters/downspouts likely need cleaning/inspection to ensure proper drainage.

items.name: Shed Exterior Paint Failure
items.description: Shed shows peeling or failing exterior paint, indicating deferred maintenance and exposure wear.
items.embed_text: Shed exterior paint failure. Peeling/flaking paint on shed. Scrape, prep, and repaint exterior.

items.name: Concrete Driveway Surface Wear
items.description: Concrete driveway shows surface wear with visible evidence of spalling, pitting, or rough patch breakdown â€” not simple aging or discoloration alone.
items.embed_text: Concrete driveway or pad surface wear. Visible spalling, pitting, or surface breakdown on driveway concrete. Stained, aged, or discolored concrete pad or driveway surface. Oil stains, weathering, or general aging on exterior concrete. Repair or resurface affected areas.

items.name: Heavy Indoor Storage/Clutter
items.description: Visible storage piles or heavy clutter reduces usable perception of space and harms listing presentation.
items.embed_text: Heavy indoor storage/clutter. Storage items and clutter visible. Declutter, organize, and clean for showings/photos.

items.name: Metal Carport Rust or Corrosion
items.description: Metal carport or similar exterior metal structure shows visible rust, corrosion, or deterioration along seams, edges, or roof panels.
items.embed_text: Metal carport rust or corrosion. Rusted or corroded metal carport structure. Weathered, aged, or deteriorated carport or outbuilding. General wear and structural aging on carport or shed exterior. Repair, treat rust, or replace affected components.

items.name: Fence Damaged or Weathered
items.description: Wood fence rotting, leaning, uneven, or deteriorated. Weathered fence boards or leaning fence sections in need of repair or replacement.
items.embed_text: Fence damaged or weathered. Fence boards or sections are rotted, leaning, uneven, faded, or deteriorated. Repair, refinish, or replace affected fencing.

items.name: Exterior Siding Discoloration or Fading
items.description: Exterior siding or trim shows fading, chalking, staining, or uneven discoloration indicating aging finishes and reduced curb appeal.
items.embed_text: Exterior siding discoloration or fading. Faded, chalky, or stained exterior siding. Lower wall staining near grade, darkened exterior siding near foundation. Repaint, refinish, or update exterior finishes.

items.name: Uneven Gravel Drive or Parking Area
items.description: Gravel driveway or parking surface appears uneven, rutted, or loosely scattered, creating potential drainage or trip concerns.
items.embed_text: Uneven gravel drive or parking area. Rutted or loose gravel surface. Regrade, compact, and refresh gravel as needed.

items.name: Dated Exterior Finishes
items.description: Overall exterior appearance feels dated due to color palette, materials, or trim details; cosmetic updates may improve curb appeal.
items.embed_text: Dated exterior finishes. Overall exterior style appears outdated. Cosmetic modernization of facade, trim, or color scheme.

items.name: Layout Modernization Opportunity
items.description: Interior layout suggests potential for modernization (e.g., opening walls, reconfiguring rooms) to improve flow and market appeal.
items.embed_text: Layout modernization opportunity. Potential to open walls or reconfigure floor plan for improved flow and appeal.

items.name: Exposed Sheathing or Missing Exterior Siding
items.description: Exterior wall area shows exposed OSB/sheathing or missing siding panels, indicating incomplete or damaged exterior cladding.
items.embed_text: Exposed sheathing or missing exterior siding. Exterior wall with visible OSB or missing cladding. Dark staining or weathering on exposed sheathing, unfinished wall section, incomplete exterior enclosure. Replace siding and properly weatherproof.

items.name: Deck Surface Weathering
items.description: Deck surface shows fading, graying, or surface wear consistent with weather exposure but not obvious structural failure.
items.embed_text: Deck surface weathering. Sun-faded or grayed wood deck boards. Clean, sand, stain, or refinish.

items.name: Tree Stump Present in Yard
items.description: Visible tree stump remaining in yard area, affecting curb appeal and potentially posing a trip hazard.
items.embed_text: Tree stump present in yard. Visible leftover stump. Remove and regrade landscaping.

items.name: Exterior Door Paint Failure
items.description: Exterior door paint appears worn, chipped, or peeling around edges, panels, or trim.
items.embed_text: Exterior door paint failure or lower door deterioration. Worn, chipped, peeling, or flaking paint on entry door. Moisture-damaged, swollen, or rotting wood at bottom of exterior door or door frame. Deteriorated door sill or threshold. Sand, prep, repair, seal, and repaint.

items.name: Dated or Worn Door Hardware
items.description: Aged lockset, brass door handles, old-style deadbolt, dated knobs or lever hardware, or worn entry hardware. Hardware replacement opportunity.
items.embed_text: Dated or worn door hardware. Door knobs, levers, locksets, or deadbolts appear aged, worn, brass, or stylistically dated. Hardware replacement opportunity.

items.name: Brick Weathering or Mortar Deterioration
items.description: Brickwork shows weathering, discoloration, or deteriorating mortar joints that may require cleaning, sealing, or repointing.
items.embed_text: Brick weathering or mortar deterioration. Discolored brick or failing mortar joints. Clean, seal, or repoint masonry.

items.name: Patio or Porch Surface Wear
items.description: Covered patio or porch surface shows aging, surface wear, or coating breakdown suggesting resurfacing or sealing.
items.embed_text: Patio or porch surface wear. Aging, worn, stained, or eroded concrete patio or porch slab. Spalled entry landing, weathered porch concrete, deteriorated patio coating or surface finish. Resurface or reseal.

items.name: Missing Base Cabinets with Exposed Subfloor/Framing
items.description: Lower cabinets are missing, exposing subflooring, framing, or plumbing areas. Indicates significant renovation required.
items.embed_text: Missing base cabinets with exposed subfloor or framing. Open or damaged under-sink cabinet area with exposed substrate, plumbing, or framing. Missing lower cabinet section, gutted base cabinetry, open cabinet void below countertop. Major cabinetry replacement and substrate repair required.

items.name: Rotted Subfloor or Structural Framing
items.description: Subflooring or framing members appear saturated, deteriorated, or structurally compromised due to prolonged moisture exposure.
items.embed_text: Rotted subfloor or structural framing. Severe moisture damage affecting load-bearing components. Structural repair required.

items.name: Interior Wall Stripped to Studs / Exposed Insulation
items.description: Interior wall has drywall removed, exposing studs and insulation, indicating incomplete renovation or severe damage.
items.embed_text: Interior wall stripped to studs with exposed insulation. Requires full drywall installation and finishing.

items.name: Boarded-Up Entry or Window
items.description: Door or window opening is boarded over, indicating damage, vacancy security measures, or structural compromise.
items.embed_text: Boarded-up entry or window. Opening secured with plywood. Replace and restore door or window assembly.

items.name: Retaining Wall Failure or Missing Section
items.description: Retaining wall section appears missing, collapsed, or structurally compromised, affecting grading and soil stability.
items.embed_text: Retaining wall failure or missing section. Structural repair required to stabilize grading and soil.

items.name: Empty or Deteriorated In-Ground Pool
items.description: In-ground pool appears empty, neglected, or visibly deteriorated, suggesting repair, restoration, or secure fill-in may be needed.
items.embed_text: Empty or deteriorated in-ground pool. Drained or neglected pool with visible deterioration or non-operational condition. Pool rehabilitation, repair, or secure removal/fill-in may be needed.

items.name: Worn or Stained Flooring
items.description: Flooring that appears worn, stained, discolored, or aged without structural concern.
items.embed_text: Worn or stained flooring. Discolored, dirty, aged, or scuffed floor surface. Cosmetic flooring wear without structural concern.

items.name: Bare or Missing Finish Flooring
items.description: Floor stripped to subfloor, concrete slab, or underlayment. Missing finish flooring or exposed subfloor with adhesive remnants. Flooring installation needed.
items.embed_text: Bare or missing finish flooring. Floor is stripped to subfloor, slab, or underlayment, or finish flooring is missing. Install finished flooring.

items.name: Dated Electrical Outlets or Switches
items.description: Older-style electrical outlets, switches, or cover plates that appear dated or discolored.
items.embed_text: Dated electrical outlets or switches. Receptacles, switches, or cover plates appear aged, yellowed, mismatched, brass, or stylistically dated. Cosmetic electrical trim update.

items.name: Dated or Basic Interior Trim
items.description: Plain, thin, or dated interior trim package including baseboards, window casing, and door casing. Builder-grade millwork suitable for modernization.
items.embed_text: Dated or builder-grade interior trim. Plain or basic baseboards, thin baseboards, older painted baseboards, dated window casing, basic window trim, plain door casing, minimal millwork, builder-grade trim, older painted window surround, basic wood trim. Trim modernization opportunity.

items.name: Dated or Basic Interior Doors
items.description: Hollow-core, six-panel, louvered, or otherwise dated interior doors that are candidates for replacement.
items.embed_text: Dated or basic interior doors. Hollow-core six-panel doors, basic builder-grade interior doors, dated wood interior doors, older painted interior doors, louvered closet doors, dated bifold closet doors, mismatched interior door styles, basic panel doors. Door replacement opportunity, not just hardware.

items.name: Dated Entry or Patio Door
items.description: Dated front, entry, storm, sliding glass, or patio door that is a candidate for replacement (the door itself, not just the hardware).
items.embed_text: Dated entry or patio door. Front, storm, sliding glass, double, or patio door unit appears aged, worn, builder-grade, or stylistically dated. Door replacement opportunity, not just hardware.

items.name: Dated Bathroom Wallpaper
items.description: Dated bathroom wallpaper, borders, or vinyl wall covering where removal and repainting would improve market appeal.
items.embed_text: Dated bathroom wallpaper. Floral, geometric, or vinyl-print wallpaper covering bathroom walls. Wallpaper borders along ceiling or above tile. Peeling or curling wallpaper edges. Vintage 80s/90s wallpaper patterns. Wallpaper removal and bathroom wall paint refresh.

items.name: Bathroom Layout Modernization Opportunity
items.description: Awkward or constrained bathroom layout where fixture relocation, vanity replacement, or broader redesign could improve function and appeal.
items.embed_text: Bathroom layout modernization opportunity. Awkward or constrained bathroom layout: corner toilet placement, narrow wedge bathroom, no vanity counter or only a pedestal sink in master, fixture clearance issues, tight shower-toilet sandwich. Layout reconfiguration or full bathroom redesign.

items.name: Bathroom Paint Refresh Recommended
items.description: Bathroom paint is intact but dated, dull, or worn enough that a cosmetic repaint would improve market appeal.
items.embed_text: Bathroom paint refresh recommended. Dated paint colors, dull or worn finish, beige or tan walls in need of a contemporary refresh. Wall paint refresh without underlying damage. Bathroom paint update.
